subject: Chemistry
question_text: ∆G = ∆G + RT lnQ V ∆G = Change in free energy as the reaction proceeds ∆G = Standard free energy change Q = Reaction quotient R = Gas constant T = Absolute temperature V Since ∆G = – RT lnK Q ∴ ∆G = – RT lnK + RT lnQ = RT ln K If Q < K, ∆G will be negative. Reaction proceeds in the forward direction. If Q = K, ∆G = 0, no net reaction. [Hint: Next relate Q with concentration of CO, H2, CH4 and H2O in view of reduced volume (increased pressure). Show that Q < K and hence the reaction proceeds in forward direction.] 103 Equilibrium 2025-26
